blurb:
This one is for the grandkids, the nieces and nephews, or the neighbor kid you are trying to pry away from a screen for a morning.

Smith-Gilbert Gardens runs this hands-on workshop for young naturalists, walking kids through how to identify insects by their body parts and what role they play in a healthy garden. It is the kind of Saturday morning that feels like play but sneaks in real learning. The Gardens are gorgeous this time of year, so adults will not be suffering through it either.

Workshops book up, so check the Gardens' site and get signed up before spots are gone.
scoring_notes: East Cobb has teens and older kids, but grandparent-age empty nesters bringing younger grandchildren is a real use case for this audience. Fits the educated, suburban profile well. Not a top-of-list pick given the audience skews older, but worth including as a family-oriented option for households with younger children or visitors.

blurb:
A free comedy show on a Saturday night in Marietta. That is the whole pitch, and it is a good one.

SoHo Kitchen and Cocktails hosts what bills itself as Atlanta's number one comedy show, and this one leans into a grown and upscale vibe. Show up, grab a drink, and settle in for a night that costs you nothing but the bar tab.

Free tickets still go fast for shows like this, so check Eventbrite and claim your spot before the weekend sneaks up on you.
scoring_notes: Free entry and a Saturday night slot work well for the adult segment of this audience. The 'grown and sexy' framing in the listing may not be the natural language of East Cobb, but the actual concept (cocktails, comedy, no cover) translates cleanly. Recurring weekly show is its biggest weakness against the uniqueness factor.

blurb:
If your Saturday mornings have been more chaotic than calm lately, this one is worth an hour and a half of your time.

Smith-Gilbert Gardens opens its grounds for a guided meditation session surrounded by trees, flowers, and the kind of quiet that is genuinely hard to find. The session explores different meditation styles, so whether you have done this before or never sat still on purpose in your life, you will find your footing. It is equal parts wellness practice and excuse to be outside somewhere beautiful.

Spots tend to fill, so check the Gardens' site and lock in your seat before the week gets away from you.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the 42-median-age crowd, especially the empty nester segment. Wellness programming at a botanical garden reads as elevated and intentional, which matches the income and education profile. Not a one-time-only event, but it is a recurring series that many readers have likely never tried. Beats a farmers market in terms of experience quality.

blurb:
Kick off the long weekend with something you almost never give yourself: a slow evening with no agenda.

Steeped in Peace is a women's gathering built around meditative coloring, warm tea, and real conversation in a room full of people who showed up for the same reason. No performance, no pressure, just a couple of hours to exhale. It is the kind of event that sounds small but tends to stick with you.

Friday night, right here in Marietta. Grab a spot and put it on the calendar before the week fills in around it.
scoring_notes: Directly targets the women in this audience, which skews toward established families and empty nesters around 42. The concept is distinctive enough to stand out from generic networking or fitness events. The Powers Ferry corridor is East Cobb-adjacent and familiar to this readership. Not a broad audience pick, but strong for the segment it serves.

event_name: Verses and Vibez Open Mic Night
blurb: Open mic nights can go either way, but Verses and Vibez is built around real variety: rap, singing, poetry, comedy, all on the same stage in one night. It's a Memorial Day week night out that doesn't require a big commitment, and it's the kind of room where you never know what you're going to get, which is half the fun. Thursday evening, Marietta, and artists who actually prepared. If you've got a curious streak and want something a little different this week, this one delivers.
scoring_notes: Fits the younger professional segment of the Perimeter audience. Memorial Day week timing helps since people tend to have more flexibility. Scores lower than other picks because open mic nights are recurring in format and the audience skew is slightly younger than the newsletter's median. Included as a solid fifth option if editors want a mid-week, lower-key pick.

event_name: The Interface: Tech and Brunch Experience
blurb: If your professional network could use some fresh faces, this is a low-pressure way to do it. The Interface is a free brunch mixer designed to connect people across Atlanta's tech world, from founders to developers to operators. It's at Timeless Kitchen and Cocktails, so the food and drinks are actually worth showing up for on their own. Sunday afternoon, no ticket required. If you work in tech or work adjacent to it, this is worth an hour of your Memorial Day weekend.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the young professional, high-education segment of the Perimeter audience. Free and on a Sunday, which is accessible. Tech networking brunch is a specific format that distinguishes it from generic mixer events. Scores slightly lower on uniqueness because networking events are common, but the brunch format and tech focus give it a clearer identity. Good pick for the professional-skewing Perimeter reader.

blurb: Not every Saturday night needs a big plan. Sometimes it's just a great room, a live saxophone, and two hours that go by too fast. Dalen Minniefield plays the kind of smooth, soulful set that works whether you come with a crowd or just one other person. Perfect Note Atlanta is an intimate venue, which means good sound, good sight lines, and none of that lost-in-a-big-room feeling. Seats will go. If a low-key but genuinely good night out sounds like your speed this weekend, this is it.
scoring_notes: Live jazz and sax fit the 38-median-age, higher-income Perimeter audience well. Perfect Note is a real venue with a good local reputation. The event is specific to this artist on this date, so it passes the uniqueness filter. Adult-skewing and low-friction for the demographic. Scores slightly lower on uniqueness because live sax nights are not uncommon in Atlanta, but the intimate venue and specific artist make it worth including.

event_name: The HEART Walk at Cochran Shoals
venue: Cochran Shoals, Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area, Marietta
blurb: Memorial Day weekend is the perfect excuse to actually use the Chattahoochee trail you've driven past a hundred times. This guided hike at Cochran Shoals pairs the walk with something a little different: a certified coach leads the group through the kind of conversation that's hard to have sitting still. It's part nature walk, part reset. Saturday late afternoon along the river, fresh air, good company. If your long weekends tend to blur into errands and screens, this is an easy way to make this one feel different.
scoring_notes: Cochran Shoals is well-known to the Perimeter audience and is a strong local hook. The guided, intentional format makes it feel different from a standard hike. Fits the young professional and adult segments well. Slightly niche positioning (neurotransformational coaching) may not land with every reader, but the outdoor angle is broadly appealing for the demographic.

event_name: Grown AF Fridays with Frank Ski at SOHO Lounge
blurb: Memorial Day weekend kicks off this Friday, and SOHO Lounge is starting it right. Frank Ski, one of Atlanta radio's most iconic voices, is behind the decks for Grown AF Fridays, a night built for adults who actually know how to have a good time. Think curated cocktails, a lively patio, and DJs who play music you recognize. Drink specials run from 7 to 10, and if you RSVP now, entry is free before 10. That's a Friday night out for the price of your first drink. Show up, let the weekend begin.
scoring_notes: Frank Ski is a genuine Atlanta institution, which adds name recognition and a can't-miss pull for the 35-45 crowd. Adult-skewing audience is a strong fit for this event. Free entry before 10 is a soft ask for a $105K median income audience. It's technically a recurring Friday series, but the Frank Ski component and Memorial Day weekend timing make it feel more special than a standard weekly night out.

venue: Sewell Mill Library and Cultural Center, Marietta
blurb: If you've ever wanted to see a play before it becomes a play, this is that. NewWorks Theatre is bringing a raw stage reading of Joel PE King's new work to Sewell Mill Library this Saturday, and it's the kind of thing that only happens once. No sets, no costumes, just actors, words, and the story doing the work. It's free, it's 90 minutes, and it's in your backyard. The educated, curious crowd this newsletter reaches tends to love exactly this kind of thing. Go see it before everyone else does.
scoring_notes: A one-time stage reading of a new work is genuinely rare and fits a high-education, culturally curious audience well. Free price point removes all friction. Saturday afternoon timing is accessible for both young professionals and empty nesters. Strong uniqueness score because a developmental reading is something most people never think to attend, and it has real cultural credibility.

event_name: Cracking You Up Comedy Open Mic
venue: Johnnie MacCracken's Celtic Firehouse Pub, Marietta
blurb: Memorial Day weekend deserves a proper sendoff, and a comedy open mic at a Celtic pub is not a bad way to do it. Cracking You Up brings together comics at every level, from first-timers to working pros, all doing five-minute sets at Johnnie MacCracken's in Marietta. You never know who is going to kill it and who is going to bomb, and that unpredictability is honestly part of the fun. It runs late, so this one is for the night owls. Go in with low expectations and you will probably leave laughing harder than you planned.
scoring_notes: Adult-skewed, low-cost, local venue. Open mic format means quality is variable, which limits how strongly it can be recommended. The Memorial Day Monday hook gives it a timely angle. Better fit for the younger end of the audience than the established empty nester crowd.

venue: Sewell Mill Library & Cultural Center, Marietta
blurb: Sewell Mill Library is hosting a half-day wellness conference designed for women who are ready to do something for themselves for a few hours. The event runs from noon to 4pm and covers mental, emotional, and personal well-being. It is right in the neighborhood, free to get to, and the kind of afternoon that is easy to write off as something you will do next time. Don't. Block the afternoon, bring a friend, and actually show up for yourself. The library has become one of the best event venues in East Cobb and this is a good reminder of that.
scoring_notes: Held at a beloved East Cobb community venue. Targets women, which skews the audience match but still relevant given the demographic mix. Afternoon timing is accessible. Scores lower on uniqueness since wellness conferences are common, but the hyper-local venue gives it a neighborhood feel.

blurb: Yoga at a distillery. With beer. Yes, that is exactly what it sounds like. Hops & Flow is a Saturday morning class at Atlanta Hard Cider & Distillery in Marietta where you move through a yoga session and then enjoy a drink after. It is lighthearted, social, and a genuinely fun way to kick off a weekend without taking yourself too seriously. Bring a friend who keeps saying they want to do more active things and then never follows through. This one is easy enough to actually make happen.
scoring_notes: Fun adult activity close to home. Recurring event on first Saturdays, which knocks the uniqueness score down. But May 23 is the upcoming first Saturday, so it's timely. Good fit for the health-conscious, socially active East Cobb crowd.

venue: Cobb Water Wildlife & Rain Garden, Marietta
blurb: If you have been meaning to do something actually useful on a Friday morning, here is your shot. Cobb Water is hosting a hands-on workshop where you build your own rain barrel from scratch and take it home with you. It connects directly to a downspout and cuts down on your water bill while keeping runoff out of local streams. East Cobb homeowners who care about their yards and their water costs will get real value out of this one. It is a Friday morning well spent.
scoring_notes: Homeownership rate of 78% makes this relevant. Practical, hands-on, and free or low cost. Appeals to the eco-conscious, home-invested crowd that makes up a big share of East Cobb. Unique enough as a make-and-take format rather than a passive presentation.

venue: Big Peach Running Co., East Cobb/Marietta
blurb: Big Peach Running Co. on Johnson Ferry Road is kicking off summer cross country miles with a free morning run hosted alongside HOKA. If you or your kid is gearing up for a fall XC season, this is a great way to start building that base with a group. East Cobb has no shortage of serious runners, and this one is right in the backyard, which makes the excuse not to go pretty thin. Show up, log some miles with good people, and get a head start on the season. Free, local, and worth getting out of bed for.
scoring_notes: Big Peach East Cobb is a neighborhood staple. The cross country angle is a strong fit for families with teen athletes, which aligns well with the established family demographic. Free entry removes any barrier. Uniqueness is moderate since it's a branded run event, but the location specificity and XC season hook make it relevant.

event_name: Classic Love: Music of Gerald LeVert
blurb: Gerald LeVert had one of the smoothest voices in R&B, and Quinn is bringing that catalog back to life for one night at Timeless Kitchen. Think slow jams, cocktails, and a room full of people who actually know every word. This is the kind of grown-up night out that is easy to let slip by if you are not paying attention. Timeless Kitchen keeps things intimate, so you are not watching from the back of a huge venue. You are right there. Grab your person, grab a table, and make a Thursday feel like a Saturday.
scoring_notes: Gerald LeVert's peak era aligns well with a median age of 42. Intimate venue, cocktail setting, and adult R&B vibe fit the empty nester and established couple crowd. Not a massive can't-miss event, but it's a specific, one-night performance rather than a recurring thing.

blurb: Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of its "A Garden with Wings" exhibit this Saturday with Pollinator Palooza, a full day of gardens, butterflies, and native plant education from 10am to 5pm. It's a genuinely beautiful property and a good slow-morning kind of outing. This one skews toward nature lovers, gardeners, and anyone who wants a reason to be outside without a crowd pressing in on all sides. The anniversary makes this year's edition worth noting. Check the Smith-Gilbert Gardens site for admission details.
scoring_notes: The 10-year anniversary of the signature exhibit gives this recurring event a genuine uniqueness bump. Skews toward the gardening and nature-curious segment of East Cobb's audience, which is real but not the majority. Good for the right reader, not a universal crowd-pleaser.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sundown Social Series – Sandy Springs**
📅 Returning this season | Sandy Springs
🎟️ Details at the link | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/24/the-future-of-sandy-springs)

The Sundown Social is back in Sandy Springs. It's an outdoor evening series — the kind of thing you show up to not quite knowing who you'll run into, and end up staying two hours longer than you planned.

For a neighborhood that's more young professionals and adults than it is school-night-bedtime households, this is exactly the right kind of event. Outdoor, social, low-commitment. You can walk in solo and leave with plans for the weekend.

Check Rough Draft Atlanta for the full schedule and dates so you don't miss the first one.
scoring_notes: The Sundown Social fits the adult-leaning, social Perimeter audience well. The series format is a slight knock on uniqueness — it's recurring — but the season return makes it timely for a newsletter publishing this week. Good match for young professionals and empty nesters who want something low-key and walkable. Lower uniqueness score reflects that it's a returning series rather than a standalone event, but the audience match is among the strongest of any candidate.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Juneteenth Celebration – City Springs**
📅 Coming to City Springs, Sandy Springs
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/27/juneteenth-celebration-city-springs/)

City Springs just announced this year's Juneteenth Celebration is coming back — and it's bigger. The event features live performances, local vendors, and expanded educational experiences that are new this year.

Juneteenth has grown into one of the most meaningful civic celebrations on the calendar, and City Springs is one of the best outdoor venues in the area to experience it. Free admission, walkable location, and a lineup built around community — it's the kind of event that reminds you why you like living here.

Details are still rolling out, so check cityssprings.com to mark your calendar before the full schedule fills in.
scoring_notes: Solid community event at a neighborhood anchor venue. Free admission removes any price barrier for an audience that wouldn't be deterred by cost anyway. The 'expanded' element gives it a fresh angle over prior years. Broad appeal across the adult and family mix in the Perimeter area. Slightly lower uniqueness score because Juneteenth events are widespread in metro Atlanta — the City Springs angle and local proximity is what elevates it for this newsletter specifically.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sandy Springs Invitational Car Show**
📅 Sunday, April 19 | Sandy Springs
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/11/artemis-ii-splashdown/)

If you've got even a passing interest in cars — classic, exotic, or just something you've never seen up close — this is worth your Sunday afternoon. The Sandy Springs Police Foundation is hosting its Invitational Car Show today, with over a hundred cars on display ranging from vintage classics to head-turning exotics. It's free, it's outside, and the proceeds support local police. It's also the kind of low-key, walk-around event that's easy to do with a coffee in hand, a kid on your hip, or just a couple of hours to kill before dinner. No tickets, no pressure — just cars and community.
scoring_notes: Happening today (April 19), so it's immediate and relevant for a newsletter publishing this week only if it goes out Sunday morning — otherwise this is stale. Good fit for the adult-skewing Perimeter audience. Free, community-benefit angle plays well with homeowners and professionals. Car shows skew male but the charity tie-in and outdoor format broaden the appeal. Uniqueness is moderate — it's a car show, but the invitational format and police foundation angle give it a purpose beyond just cars.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Dunwoody Art Festival**
📅 May 9-10 | Various times | Dunwoody
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://festivalguidesandreviews.com/georgia-festivals/)

Mark your calendars for the Dunwoody Art Festival next month — it's one of those rare events that manages to be both sophisticated and completely accessible. Local and regional artists will showcase everything from paintings and photography to jewelry and ceramics, all set up right in the heart of Dunwoody. This isn't your typical craft fair. The quality bar is high, the crowd is engaged, and you might actually find something worth hanging on your wall. With 70% of Perimeter residents holding bachelor's degrees or higher, this audience appreciates good art and has the disposable income to buy it. It's free to attend, runs all weekend, and gives you a chance to discover new artists while supporting the local arts community. Plus, you can walk there.
scoring_notes: Excellent demographic fit - highly educated, affluent audience that appreciates arts and culture. Annual event but still worthwhile for its quality and local relevance. Appeals to adult sophistication while remaining family-friendly. Free admission removes any barrier.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Atlanta Dogwood Festival**
📅 Saturday, April 10 | All Day | Piedmont Park, Atlanta
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/10/too-loud/)

The 90th Annual Atlanta Dogwood Festival is happening this weekend, and that number tells you something. This festival has staying power because it delivers what Atlanta springs are all about — perfect weather, blooming trees, and the whole city coming out to celebrate. Piedmont Park transforms into a massive outdoor party with live music on multiple stages, local artists selling their work, and food vendors serving everything from barbecue to funnel cakes. The Kid's Alley keeps families happy with activities and games. What sets this apart from smaller neighborhood festivals is the scale and quality — national touring acts, serious local musicians, and vendors who've earned their spot over decades. It's a 20-minute drive from Perimeter, but you're getting Atlanta's premier spring festival for free.
scoring_notes: Major Atlanta institution with 90-year history creates strong uniqueness score. Appeals to educated, culturally-engaged demographic. Free admission removes price barrier. Distance from Perimeter and large crowds may be deterrents for some suburban residents.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sandy Springs Invitational Car Show**
📅 Saturday, April 19 | All Day | Sandy Springs
🎟️ $15 | [Get Tickets](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/12/sandy-springs-car-show-fundraiser/)

This isn't your typical parking lot car show. The Sandy Springs Invitational brings together classic muscle cars, exotic sports cars, and everything in between for a full day of automotive eye candy. What makes it worth the drive is the mix — you'll see a 1967 Camaro parked next to a brand-new McLaren. The event benefits the Sandy Springs Police Foundation, so your ticket helps fund community programs while you browse some serious horsepower. Food trucks handle lunch, and there's usually live music to keep the energy up. It's the kind of Saturday where you plan to stay an hour and end up there all afternoon. Car enthusiast or not, there's something satisfying about seeing these machines up close.
scoring_notes: Strong appeal for Perimeter's affluent demographic with interest in luxury items. Annual event but unique enough to be noteworthy. $15 price point is very accessible for $105K median income households. Mix of classic and exotic cars appeals to broad age range.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Lemonade Days Festival**
📅 Wednesday, April 22 | All Day | Downtown Dunwoody
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.atlantaonthecheap.com/lemonade-days-festival-dunwoody/)

Dunwoody's biggest party of the year kicks off Wednesday and runs through Sunday. This is the 26th year of Lemonade Days, which means they've figured out how to do it right. Five straight days of live music, food trucks, local vendors, and activities that work for everyone from toddlers to grandparents. The festival takes over downtown Dunwoody, so you can walk between stages, grab dinner, and let the kids run around without driving anywhere. It's the kind of community event that makes you remember why you moved to the suburbs in the first place. The lineup changes daily, so you can come back multiple times and catch something different each night.
scoring_notes: Perfect fit for Perimeter's mixed demographic of young professionals and families. Five-day duration creates multiple opportunities to attend. Annual tradition with strong community appeal that matches suburban lifestyle preferences.
